= flexCorrect (Appium Self-Healing)
:navtitle: flexCorrect

`flexCorrect` is Kobiton's Appium Self-Healing capability. It selects the correct element across different devices and when layouts change between runs, reducing `Element not found` errors during automation testing.

== How flexCorrect works

`flexCorrect` supports two Xium automation script types:

* A Xium automation script xref:automation-testing:scripting/auto-generate-an-appium-script.adoc[generated by Kobiton AI] from a manual session.
* A custom (non-generated) Xium automation script.

`flexCorrect` requires a baseline automation session before `flexCorrect` can run — see <<_prepare_a_baseline_session>>.

During execution, if an element's layout changes, `flexCorrect` uses AI to locate the element and continue the test instead of returning `Element not found`.

[#_prepare_a_baseline_session]
== Prepare a baseline session

. Run a manual session on a Kobiton device without `kobiton:flexCorrect` enabled. Note the manual session ID.
. Use the manual session ID as the value for `kobiton:baselineSessionId` when you configure capabilities.

== Configure the capability

Add the following capability to your script:

[cols="1,1"]
|===
| Capability | Value

| `kobiton:flexCorrect`
| `true`
|===

Example (Java):

[source,java]
----
capabilities.setCapability("kobiton:flexCorrect", true);
capabilities.setCapability("kobiton:baselineSessionId", "0000011");
----

> Replace `0000011` with the manual session ID for the baseline session.

Save your changes and run the script.

== Limitations

`flexCorrect` is not available in the following session types:

* xref:automation-testing:start-a-mixed-session.adoc[Mixed sessions]
* xref:automation-testing:basic-appium-server/launch-a-basic-appium-2-session.adoc[Basic Appium 2 sessions]

== Best practices

* Keep `flexCorrect` enabled as a fallback. If the script finds the expected element, `flexCorrect` does not change the session behavior.
* Use a manual session ID as the baseline session ID.
* Run the baseline session on a device with the layouts you expect `flexCorrect` to handle in later test runs.

[#_review_correction_results]
== Review correction results

After a session runs, review the Appium self-healing validation results in the session details. The results show which elements were corrected by `flexCorrect`.

.Appium Self-Healing issue count in Session Explorer
image::automation-testing:flexcorrect-count.png[width=750,alt="Session Explorer showing the Appium Self-Healing issue count"]

.Details preview shown when hovering over an issue
image::automation-testing:flexcorrect-details.png[width=750,alt="Pointer hovering over an Appium Self-Healing issue to show a details preview"]

[NOTE]
After a session, you can check xref:session-explorer:analytics/review-test-result-analysis.adoc#_test_result_analysis_overview[how many elements were corrected,window=read-later].
